# browser-agent-cli

> Real browser automation for AI coding agents — launch Chrome Beta with a dedicated agent profile and control it via Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). No headless mode. Persistent logins. Isolated from the user's personal browser.

## Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/nolan-vale/browser-agent-cli.git
cd browser-agent-cli && bash install.sh
npm install -g chrome-devtools-mcp
```

Requires: macOS, Google Chrome Beta.

## Commands

- `chrome-beta-agent [url]` — launch Chrome Beta with agent profile + CDP on port 9222; if already running, opens url in a new tab
- `chrome-beta-agent-stop` — gracefully stop the agent browser
- `chrome-devtools <cmd>` — control the browser via CDP (from `chrome-devtools-mcp` npm package)

## Full stack

```
chrome-beta-agent <url>  →  http://127.0.0.1:9222  →  chrome-devtools <command>
```

## Core chrome-devtools commands

- `chrome-devtools take_snapshot` — a11y tree of the page, returns element UIDs
- `chrome-devtools click "uid"` — click an element
- `chrome-devtools fill "uid" "text"` — type into an input
- `chrome-devtools navigate_page --url "https://..."` — navigate
- `chrome-devtools take_screenshot` — screenshot of the viewport
- `chrome-devtools list_console_messages` — browser console log
- `chrome-devtools list_network_requests` — network tab
- `chrome-devtools evaluate_script "() => document.title"` — run JS

## Best for

- AI coding agents that need a real browser with persistent logins
- Automating web apps, dashboards, and login-protected content
- Browser UI testing with visible feedback
- Agents that need to observe, not just scrape

## Why real browser (not headless)

- Persistent logins across sessions — no re-auth
- Not blocked by anti-bot systems
- Visible — easy to observe and intervene
- Dedicated agent profile keeps agent sessions separate from user's Chrome

## Skill for Claude Code / Codex

`skills/SKILL.md` — copy to `~/.claude/skills/chrome-devtools-cli/SKILL.md` and `~/.codex/skills/chrome-devtools-cli/SKILL.md`. `install.sh` does this automatically.

## Safety

Agents must ask before: form submit, send, delete, upload, account/billing changes.
Agents must stop for: login, CAPTCHA, MFA, passkey, SMS/email verification.

## Key files

- `README.md` — overview and usage
- `AGENTS.md` — for AI agents
- `bin/chrome-beta-agent` — launcher script
- `bin/chrome-beta-agent-stop` — stop script
- `skills/SKILL.md` — Claude Code / Codex skill

- GitHub: https://github.com/nolan-vale/browser-agent-cli
- chrome-devtools CLI: https://www.npmjs.com/package/chrome-devtools-mcp
- CDP reference: https://chromedevtools.github.io/devtools-protocol/
